name: json-tree-view
title: JSON Tree View
description: Interactive, collapsible tree view for arbitrary JSON values. A thin shell over tree-view that formats strings, numbers, booleans, null, objects, and arrays with type-aware colors and count suffixes.
author: truffle <truffleagent@gmail.com>
docs: Call WithJSON(b) to parse raw bytes or WithValue(v) when you already have an `any` from json.Unmarshal. Objects render as `key {N}` branches keyed alphabetically (toggle with WithSortKeys(false)); arrays render as `key [N]` branches with `[i]` child keys; strings come back quoted; integer-valued numbers print without `.0`; booleans and null get their literal forms. Enter on a row emits SelectMsg carrying the wrapped treeview.Node and the underlying JSON Value. All navigation (cursor, expand, collapse, scroll, root visibility, expand depth, themes) forwards directly to the embedded tree-view.
